name: Aerobic training increased mitochondrial biogenesis and dynamics markers in older men
summary: A small human training study directly links aerobic exercise to mitochondrial biogenesis and fusion/fission proteins in older skeletal muscle.
statement: In sedentary young and older men, 12 weeks of supervised aerobic cycling increased skeletal-muscle PGC-1alpha, oxidative enzymes, MFN1, MFN2, and FIS1, with VO2 peak and quadriceps cross-sectional area also increasing after training.
population_or_model: Young men around 20 years old and older men around 74 years old
quantitative_note: In older men, the source reports PGC-1alpha +55%, citrate synthase +65%, MFN1 +93%, MFN2 +36%, and FIS1 +201% after aerobic exercise training.
caveats: The older-adult group was small, with six older men.
Protein-marker movement is not the same as durable clinical benefit.
The study did not test a pharmacologic biogenesis or dynamics intervention.
